subject: Mathematics
note: Achievement standards from the Australian Curriculum Version 9. Individual year levels (not 2-year bands like NZC). Strands: Number, Algebra, Measurement, Space, Statistics, Probability. Verify against australiancurriculum.edu.au.

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Subitise small collections; count objects to at least 20; connect number names, numerals, and quantities; compare and order collections

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Count to and from 120; partition numbers using place value; addition and subtraction within 20; recognise and describe simple fractions (halves and quarters)

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Count to 1000; use and represent numbers flexibly; mental strategies for addition and subtraction to 100; basic multiplication and division concepts; simple fractions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Read, write, and order numbers to 10,000; multiplication and division facts to 10×10; add and subtract two- and three-digit numbers; model fractions and decimals

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply place value to millions; use efficient strategies for multi-digit multiplication and division; compare, order, and represent fractions and decimals; connect fractions, decimals, and percentages

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Measure and compare length, area, mass, capacity, and time using standard units; estimate area using arrays; calculate perimeter of rectangles

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply strategies for multiplication and division of larger numbers; compute with fractions, decimals, and percentages; use ratios and proportional reasoning

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Describe and continue numerical and geometric patterns; write and evaluate simple algebraic expressions; use number properties to solve problems

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply operations with fractions, decimals, and percentages; use ratios and rates to solve problems; introduce integers on a number line

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Collect, display, and interpret data in a range of representations; calculate mean, median, and mode; interpret probability as a value between 0 and 1

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply operations with integers and rational numbers; solve problems involving fractions, decimals, percentages, ratios, and rates; use index notation

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Generate sequences; simplify algebraic expressions; apply the four operations to algebraic terms; introduce linear equations

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Classify triangles and quadrilaterals; use properties of angles formed by parallel lines and transversals; apply transformations on the Cartesian plane

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply the four operations with rational numbers; solve problems involving percentages, ratios, and rates in context; introduce scientific notation

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Solve linear equations and inequalities; plot linear relations on the Cartesian plane; explore gradient and y-intercept

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply Pythagoras' theorem; calculate areas of composite shapes; calculate volumes of prisms and cylinders; convert between units

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply operations with real numbers including surds; use scientific notation; solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Expand binomial products; factorise algebraic expressions; solve quadratic equations graphically and algebraically; explore non-linear functions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply trigonometric ratios to right-angled triangles; use similarity and congruence in geometric proofs; explore properties of circles

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply operations with real numbers and surds; use logarithms; solve problems involving compound interest and financial planning

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Factorise and expand more complex algebraic expressions; solve quadratic and exponential equations; graph a range of functions; introduce polynomial and logarithmic functions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Analyse bivariate data using scatter plots and lines of best fit; calculate and interpret statistical measures; evaluate probability including conditional probability
